description: k6 smoke and load test observability for the microservices platform. Tracks throughput, latency percentiles, check pass rates, and per-phase HTTP timing for every test run — scoped by environment, runner engine, workload profile, and committed preset.

options.content:
## k6 Observability — what this board shows & how to read it
k6 runs the **same parametrized script** (`microservices-smoke.js`) from Jenkins, Tekton or GitHub Actions and exports metrics over **OpenTelemetry** (service `k6-microservices-smoke`). Counters are **per-run and ephemeral** — they only carry data for the window of an actual run, so **widen the time range** to cover one. See [`docs/302`](../docs/302-K6_LOAD_TESTING.md).

**Filters (top variables):** **`deployment_environment`** = which tier was tested (stable | develop) · **`ci_runner`** = engine that ran it (`gha` / `jenkins` / `tekton`) · **`k6_profile`** = workload shape · **`k6_preset`** = committed config (or `none` for ad-hoc). If a panel is empty, no run matches the current filters — broaden them or launch **`Day2.traffic.01-k6`**.

### Dashboard sections at a glance
| Section | What it answers |
|---|---|
| **Run summary** | Single-number KPIs for the current run: iterations, RPS, pass rate, latency, VUs, data |
| **Throughput, VUs & iterations** | Request rate over time, VU ramp shape, and iteration throughput |
| **Latency & percentiles** | p50/p95/p99 trends and per-request latency breakdown |
| **HTTP request timing breakdown** | Phase decomposition: blocked / connecting / TLS / waiting (TTFB) / receiving |
| **Checks & errors** | Failed request rate, check pass/fail rate, per-check pass counts |
| **Data transfer** | Bytes/s received and sent |
| **Traces & logs** | Trace list for the test run and live pod logs from the target service |

### Workload profiles — *the shape of the load* (`K6SIM_PROFILE`)
| Profile | Executor | Default shape | Use it to… |
|---|---|---|---|
| **smoke** *(default)* | shared-iterations | 4 VUs share 12 iters | prove the pipes + feed Grafana. **Not** a load test |
| **load** | ramping-vus | 0→20 (30s) · hold 2m · →0 | behaviour at **expected steady** traffic |
| **stress** | ramping-vus | →50 (1m) →100 (2m) · hold 2m | push **past normal** to find where it degrades |
| **soak** | constant-vus | 10 VUs for **1h** | catch **leaks / drift** over a long flat run |
| **spike** | ramping-vus | 0→100 (10s) · hold 1m · →0 | **sudden burst** — autoscaling + recovery |
| **breakpoint** | ramping-arrival-rate | 1→200 req/s over 5m, **aborts on breach** | find the **capacity knee** (stops when p95 budget breaks) |

### Committed presets — *ready-to-run configs* (`preset` input → `presets/*.yaml`)
| Preset | Lvl | Profile | Shape (VUs/rate · dur) | p95 / err budget | Use case |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **smoke** | 🟢 | smoke | 4 VUs × 12 iters | 3000ms / 5% | post-deploy "is it flowing?" (pipeline k6 stage) |
| **load-baseline** | 🟢 | load | →20 VUs · 5m | 2000ms / 2% | steady expected-load SLO (nightly/pre-merge) |
| **frontend-only** | 🟢 | load | 10 VUs · 3m (ui+proxy) | 2500ms / 2% | focus the **edge/gateway** path |
| **develop-smoke** | 🟢 | smoke | 4 VUs × 12 (develop ns) | 3000ms / 5% | validate / drive the **develop** tier |
| **stress-peak** | 🔵 | stress | 50→**100** VUs · hold 2m | 5000ms / 10% | capacity at ~2× load; watch the tail |
| **spike-recovery** | 🔵 | spike | →**100** VUs · 1m · sharp drop | 5000ms / 15% | flash-crowd **elasticity + recovery** (HPA) |
| **soak-endurance** | 🔵 | soak | 10 VUs · **1h** | 3000ms / 5% | **leak/drift** hunting over time |
| **rps-steady** | 🔵 | arrival-rate | **120 req/s** · 5m | 2000ms / 2% | **throughput** sign-off (open model) |
| **breakpoint-capacity** | 🔵 | breakpoint | →**400 req/s** · 8m · **aborts at knee** | 1500ms / 10% | find the **capacity ceiling** |

### How to read the results
- 🟢 **Newcomer** — *Checks pass rate* + *HTTP req failed*: did it pass and stay within the error budget?
- 🟡 **Operator** — *p95 latency* vs the preset budget, *failed rate* vs the err budget: which threshold breached?
- 🔵 **Specialist** — **tail**: `p99`/`max` ≫ `p95` ⇒ bad outliers · **server vs network**: in *Request timing breakdown*, **waiting (TTFB) ≈ total** ⇒ server-bound, a large *blocked/connecting/tls* gap ⇒ network/connection-bound · **throughput vs VUs** flattening ⇒ saturation · rising VUs with flat RPS ⇒ the system is the bottleneck.

> All latency metrics are in **milliseconds**. `0`/empty = no run for the selected filters/time-range (not a fault).

panels.description: Total completed iterations for the run. Compare with the configured target in the preset.
panels.title: Iterations

panels.description: Completed iterations per second.
panels.title: Iteration rate

panels.description: Total HTTP requests sent. Includes all named requests across all scenarios.
panels.title: Requests

panels.description: HTTP requests per second averaged over the run.
panels.title: Request rate (RPS)

panels.description: Percentage of k6 checks that passed. Below the preset budget (e.g. 95–100%) indicates assertion failures.

targets.expr: 100 * (sum(increase(checks_total{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset", condition="nonzero"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) / clamp_min(sum(increase(checks_total{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__range])),1)

panels.title: Checks pass rate

panels.description: Fraction of requests that triggered k6's http_req_failed flag. 0% is ideal; compare against the preset error budget.

targets.expr: 100 * (sum(increase(http_req_failed_total{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset", condition="nonzero"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) / clamp_min(sum(increase(http_req_failed_total{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__range])),1)

panels.title: HTTP req failed rate

panels.description: Median HTTP request duration for the run. Low sensitivity to outliers — use p95/p99 for SLO assessment.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(http_req_duration_milliseconds_bucket{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__range])))

panels.title: Latency p50

panels.description: 95th-percentile HTTP request duration. Primary SLO metric — compare against the preset budget.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le) (rate(http_req_duration_milliseconds_bucket{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__range])))

panels.title: Latency p95

panels.description: 99th-percentile HTTP request duration. Catches the worst 1% of requests — useful for tail-latency sensitive workloads.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le) (rate(http_req_duration_milliseconds_bucket{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__range])))

panels.title: Latency p99

panels.description: Maximum concurrently active virtual users reached during the run.
panels.title: Peak VUs

panels.description: Total bytes received from the server during the run.
panels.title: Data received

panels.description: Total bytes sent to the server during the run.
panels.title: Data sent

panels.description: HTTP request rate broken down by request name and HTTP status code. Shows which endpoints are busy and which are failing.
panels.title: Request rate by name + status

panels.description: Active VUs over time vs the configured maximum. Ramp shape reflects the profile executor.
panels.title: Virtual users (active vs max)

panels.description: Iteration completions per second. Flat or declining despite constant VUs indicates back-pressure.
panels.title: Iterations /s

panels.description: 95th and 99th-percentile full iteration duration (all requests in one logical loop). Higher than http_req_duration indicates sequential wait or think-time.
panels.title: Iteration duration p95 / p99

panels.description: p50, p95, and p99 request duration over time. Diverging p99 vs p95 indicates emerging tail latency.
panels.title: Request duration p50 / p95 / p99

panels.description: 95th-percentile duration per named request. Identifies which endpoint drives the tail.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, name) (rate(http_req_duration_milliseconds_bucket{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: p95 duration by request

panels.description: 99th-percentile duration per named request. Use alongside p95 to assess outlier severity.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, name) (rate(http_req_duration_milliseconds_bucket{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: p99 duration by request

panels.description: Mean duration per named request. Pair with percentiles to detect skewed distributions.

targets.expr: sum by (name) (rate(http_req_duration_milliseconds_sum{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__rate_interval])) / clamp_min(sum by (name) (rate(http_req_duration_milliseconds_count{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset"}[$__rate_interval])),1)

panels.title: Avg duration by request
HTTP request timing breakdown (where the time goes — specialist)

panels.description: waiting≈total ⇒ server-bound; a gap ⇒ time spent in connect/TLS/network.
panels.title: Server TTFB (waiting) vs total — p95

panels.description: p95 time for DNS lookup, TCP connect, and TLS handshake phases. Non-zero blocking = connection pool exhaustion or cold starts.
panels.title: Connection phases p95 (blocked / connecting / TLS)

panels.description: p95 time to send the request body and receive the full response. High receive time = large payloads or slow streaming.
panels.title: Send / receive p95

panels.description: Average time per phase stacked across the full request lifecycle. Reveals where the overall latency budget is consumed.
panels.title: Full phase stack — avg (blocked+connecting+tls+sending+waiting+receiving)

panels.description: k6's http_req_failed (a request failing its expected-status/check logic). 0 = all requests OK.

targets.expr: (sum(rate(http_req_failed_total{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset", condition="nonzero"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0))

panels.title: HTTP failed requests /s

panels.description: Pass and total check events per second. A widening gap between total and pass = increasing assertion failures.
panels.title: Checks pass vs total /s

panels.description: Per-check passing count across the selected range.

targets.expr: sum by (check, scenario) (increase(checks_total{service_name="k6-microservices-smoke", deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", ci_runner=~"$ci_runner", k6_profile=~"$k6_profile", k6_preset=~"$k6_preset", condition="nonzero"}[$__range]))

panels.title: Checks — pass count by check (over range)

panels.description: Bytes per second received from the service under test. Spike patterns correlate with large response payloads.
panels.title: Data received /s

panels.description: Bytes per second sent to the service under test.
panels.title: Data sent /s

panels.description: Traces emitted while the test ran (the script propagates trace context). Click a Trace ID → trace view.
panels.title: k6 test traces

panels.description: App logs from the tier under test. Filter by $level.
panels.title: Microservices pod logs (target of the test)

title: CI-CD / k6 Observability
